title: NH athlete is running Boston for the care that keeps her breathing
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Alison Clode, from Portsmouth, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Mass Eye and Ear.
Clode was treated for subglottic stenosis by Dr. Song at Mass Eye and Ear, improving her breathing.
She aims to raise funds for research on breathing difficulties associated with various medical conditions.

title: A major North Shore office park is adding 60 new condos
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cummings Properties is adding 60 condominiums at Cumming Center in Beverly, with nearly half already claimed by buyers.
The Beverly Landing development, targeting 55-plus homeowners, reserves 80% of units for households with older residents.
Condo sizes range from 700 to 2,600 square feet with prices between $500,000 and $1.5 million, including nine subsidized units.

title: Connelly Early named to Red Sox starting rotation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Connelly Early named to Red Sox starting rotation for 2026, starting third game against the Reds.
Early, a 23-year-old left-hander, debuted in 2025 with 11 strikeouts, tying a Red Sox rookie debut record.
Boston's Johan Oviedo will shift to bullpen; Garrett Crochet and Sonny Gray start first two games.

title: Experience an ‘unforgettable’ Easter weekend trip in this Maine city, according to Booking.com
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Portland, Maine, is named one of 15 unforgettable Easter weekend trips in the U.S. by Booking.com.
Travelers in Portland can explore the scenic waterfront, historic Old Port, and enjoy local seafood and lighthouse views.
Portland hotels received Michelin keys in 2025, and local chefs are James Beard 2026 semifinalists.

title: Netflix star Emma Hernan is opening a frozen food facility in Hingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Emma Hernan of 'Selling Sunset' opens a $4.6 million frozen food facility in Hingham for Coast to Coast Kitchen.
The 28,000-square-foot Hingham plant will produce crab cakes and shrimp spring rolls, employing around 50 people.
Hernan's new facility will prioritize hiring local women, continuing her family's South Shore food manufacturing presence.

title: This mental health worker is running Boston for her neighbors in South Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sarah Clark is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for South Boston Neighborhood House.
The organization, known as The Ollie, provides crucial services like early education, job support, and housing security.
Clark works with Alliance for Inclusion and Prevention, supporting youth mental health in Boston-area schools.

title: Giveaway: Enter to win tickets to ‘The Drama’
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Win tickets to 'The Drama' screening starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson by submitting a relationship question to Love Letters.
Submit questions from March 24, 10 a.m., to March 27, 11 a.m., for a chance to win.
The exclusive screening takes place Sunday evening in Somerville; winners notified by 5 p.m. Friday.

title: Lowell was named the best place to live in Mass. But why?
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lowell was named the best place to live in Massachusetts and the No. 3 in the Northeast by Livability.
The city is praised for its strong sense of community, vibrant arts scene, and expanding opportunities, supported by local collaboration.
Lowell is investing in education with institutions like UMass Lowell and projects like the Lowell Innovation Network Corridor.

title: Body of missing Holy Cross student recovered in Connecticut lake
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The body of Dominick Tocci, a 20-year-old Holy Cross student, was found in Lake Hayward, Connecticut, after a canoe accident.
Tocci and another student capsized on Lake Hayward; the other student was rescued and treated at a hospital.
DEEP Environmental Conservation Police are investigating the accident; the Medical Examiner will determine the cause of death.

title: Livestream: Testimony resumes in Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: John Waller
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, is on trial for assault with a dangerous weapon after being shot at home.
Fitzsimmons pleaded not guilty; the trial is broadcast live via NBC10 Boston.
The incident occurred last summer when Fitzsimmons was shot by a colleague in her residence.
